response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings 15+ years of genuine full-stack engineering depth, a strong open-source track record (GISCollective, multiple OSS libraries, a FOSS4G talk), and demonstrable software craftsmanship practices. However, the role carries a hard German language requirement at C1 minimum, and the candidate self-reports only 'Basic' German — a disqualifying gap for a client-facing Head of Development role in a German IT consulting firm. Python and UML, both listed as desired qualifications, are absent from the profile, and there is no clear evidence of managing a development team, owning project delivery end-to-end, or operating at the executive seniority this role demands.
strengths: 15+ years of hands-on engineering across web, embedded, mobile, and high-throughput distributed systems demonstrates genuine technical breadth
Strong open-source ethos backed by shipped projects (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial, Vibe.dav) and a FOSS4G conference presentation
Consistent application of software craftsmanship — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing — across multiple employers
concerns: German proficiency is self-reported as 'Basic', far below the mandatory C1 minimum required for client advisory and leadership work in a German consulting context
Python and UML are both absent from the skill set and resume, despite being listed as desired qualifications for this role
No explicit evidence of team management, headcount responsibility, project P&L ownership, or executive-level client advisory — the core duties of this Head of Dev position

key_factors.factor: German language gap (Basic vs. required C1)
No team leadership or management track record
